audit_items.proof_trace: lru_crawler_close_client is a 5-line linear function with no branches and no allocations of its own. On its single path it transfers ownership of c->c via sidethread_conn_close (which calls redispatch_conn, returning the conn to its worker thread for teardown) and frees c->buf directly. All four call sites in lru_crawler_write are guarded by an early-return null check (L381), so c->c is always valid when the function executes.
